content: State-of-the-art 2024–2025 active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as evolving "collective agents" where individual memory slots behave like semi-autonomous curious sub-agents competing to be retrieved for new queries, rather than passive stored data. This means curiosity-driven exploration (from ICM/RND frameworks) does not just guide the top-level agent’s data selection—it shapes internal competition between existing memory entries to prune and update the agent’s knowledge store.
